description: Bifolio of communal accounting. Dating: maybe 12th century. The hands may be known (verso is in a different hand than recto). Very neatly written. Divided by week (Shabbat צו, Shabbat ויהי ביום השמיני, Shabbat אמור, etc.). Each week includes sub-entries including "rusūm" (fees?") and "khubz" (bread for the poor). Some also say "iqtaraḍa" (borrowed). Names include: Yiṣḥaq and his wife, the mother of R. Efrayim, a sick man (marīḍ min ahl al-balad), a stranger (gharīb), a stranger known as al-Najīb, and Ibn al-Parnas al-Dimashqī.
description_he: רשימה או טבלה בHebrew numerals, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
